zh.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten是Luitgarde van Cuijk的女兒，於大約1187年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特布雷達。她和Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE至少有6個兒子和7個女兒。她去世於1237年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten是Luitgarde van Cuijk的女儿，于大约1187年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特布雷達。她和Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE至少有6个儿子和7个女儿。她去世于1237年之后。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Breda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Dino, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Geno, Massimo, Quintino, Sante, Ugo.

Italian: topographic name from medieval Latin braida ‘grassy meadow, flat grassland adjoining a settlement’, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, such as the districts (now suburbs) of Genoa, Milan, Mantua, and Brescia called Breda or Brera.
Dutch: habitational name fromthe city of this name in North Brabant.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 任, based on its Mandarin pronunciation, see   Ren   1.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 全, based on its pronunciation in Shanghai dialect, see   Quan   2.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 曾, see   Zeng  .

variants.name: Ren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Yong, Hong, Hui, Chuan, Gang, Guang, Jian, Liang, Lihua, Ning, Qing, Chang, Min, Shen, Yiming.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 任, a term for women's rank of peerage in ancient Chinese: (i) it is said that the surname can be traced back to one of the 25 sons of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from Ren (任), the name of an ancient state (located in Renxian in Hebei province) granted to Yu Yang, said to be a son or grandson of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). His descendants adopted the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from Ren (任), the name of a state (located in Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).
English: variant of   Renn   2.

nameMeanings.name: Schooten
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Dirk, Bastian, Berend, Gradus, Harmen, Hendrick, Jacobus, Willem, Wim.
Dutch and German: patronymic from the status name Scholte ‘village headman’, a variant of Schulte .

variants.name: Schulte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Dieter, Heinz, Rainer, Alois, Dietmar, Franz, Fritz, Gerhard, Hans.

North German and Dutch: status name from Middle Low German schulthete, Middle Dutch schulte ‘village headman’. In the northwestern Germany the name denotes the holder of a large farm. His status came with the obligation to make certain legal decisions. Compare Schulz , Schultheis .

variants.name: Scholtens
meanings: Dutch: variant of   Scholten  .

nameMeanings.name: Sophia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek word meaning ‘wisdom’. The Eastern cult of  St Sophia  arose as a result of misinterpretation of the phrase Hagia Sophia ‘holy wisdom’ as if it meant ‘St Sophia’. The name became popular in England in the 17th and 18th centuries—the heroine of  Fielding's  novel Tom Jones (  1749 ) is called  Sophia Weston—and  has been increasing in popularity since the 1990s. In Scotland it has been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Beathag .

variants.name: Sofia
meanings: variant spelling of   Sophia .

en.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten was born about 1187, in Breda, North Brabant, Netherlands as the daughter of Luitgarde van Cuijk. She had at least 6 sons and 7 daughters with Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE. She died after 1237.

fr.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten est née environ 1187, à Breda, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, fille de Luitgarde van Cuijk. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles avec Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE. Elle est décédée après 1237.

de.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten wurde um 1187 in Breda, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande, als Tochter von Luitgarde van Cuijk geboren. Sie hatte mit Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1237.

it.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten è nata il circa 1187 nel Breda, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi come figlia di Luitgarde van Cuijk. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie con Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE. Morì dopo il 1237.

ja.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schootenは約1187年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントブレダでLuitgarde van Cuijkの娘として生まれた。彼女はRasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVEとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1237年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten(은)는 대략 1187년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 브레다에서 Luitgarde van Cuijk의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1237년 이후 사망했다.

pt.Breda, North Brabant, Netherlands: Breda, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten nasceu aproximadamente 1187, em Breda, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos filha de Luitgarde van Cuijk. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas com Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE. Ela faleceu após 1237.

ru.lifeSummary: Sophia van Breda en Schooten родилась около 1187 г. в местности бреда, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды. Её мать звали Luitgarde van Cuijk. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей от Rasse IX "dit L'Ainé" de GRAVE. Она умерла после 1237 г.
